commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blockers are contributor real-behavior proof and maintainer session-state policy acceptance, not a narrow automated code repair on the PR branch.; Cleared: No dependency, workflow, secret, permission, package, or supply-chain change was found; tool replay remains tracked as functional session-state merge risk. (sha=34f81f04abc3601aa73bf8f68365da6c071d6ace)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes main-session restart recovery to resume non-resumable assistant-tail interruptions by re-presenting the last user message, adding a prompt-level idempotency guard for detected tool calls, widening fallback user-message lookup, and adding regression tests.

PR surface: Source +154, Tests +324. Total +478 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main and v2026.6.10 still fail-close non-resumable assistant tails, and the PR tests model the new assistant-tail recovery cases. I did not run a live gateway restart in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are contributor real-behavior proof and maintainer session-state policy acceptance, not a narrow automated code repair on the PR branch.

target.title: [AI] fix(agents): recover from non-resumable assistant tail by re-presenting last user message
target.body:
## Summary

Gateway restart recovery for non-resumable assistant tails now re-presents the last user transcript message with an idempotency guard when pending tool calls are detected, instead of forcing a user retype. Both `hasAssistantToolCalls()` and `findLastUserMessage()` now handle OpenClaw canonical content-block formats in addition to raw API formats.

- Problem: After gateway restart, sessions with assistant-tail interruptions failed silently, forcing users to retype. The recovery code only handled raw API message formats, missing canonical content-block representations.
- Solution: Detect pending assistant tool calls in both raw `tool_calls[]` and content-block formats (`toolCall`/`toolUse`/`functionCall`). Extract user message text from both string content and content-block arrays. Inject idempotency guard when tool calls are detected.
- What changed: `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.ts` — `hasAssistantToolCalls()` canonical content-block detection, `findLastUserMessage()` content-block array text extraction via `collectTextContentBlocks()`, `buildResumeMessage()` idempotency guard
- What did NOT change: Resumable tail paths (user/tool/toolResult), approval-pending tail recovery, auto-resume session detection

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #95609
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

When the gateway restarts and a main session's transcript tail is a non-resumable assistant message, the current behavior silently fails the session. The user must retype their request from scratch, even though the original request is preserved in the session transcript. This PR recovers from that state by re-presenting the last user message and continuing from the existing transcript.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: Non-resumable assistant-tail recovery now (1) detects pending tool calls in both raw `tool_calls[]` and canonical content-block formats, (2) extracts user message text from both string and content-block array formats, and (3) injects an idempotency guard when pending tool calls are found.

- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node v24.13.1, commit `2bfa8992` (content-block user message fix)

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# Full test suite (42 tests)
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts --run

# Runtime proof: hasAssistantToolCalls with 8 canonical message shapes
node --import tsx --input-type=module -e '...'
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts --run

 RUN  v4.1.8

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  42 passed (42)
   Start at  12:00:11
   Duration  6.11s
```

Direct runtime proof — `hasAssistantToolCalls()` canonical content-block detection:
```console
PASS [content-block toolCall]: got=true expected=true
PASS [content-block toolUse]: got=true expected=true
PASS [raw tool_calls]: got=true expected=true
PASS [text-only]: got=false expected=false
PASS [mixed content text+toolCall]: got=true expected=true
PASS [empty content array]: got=false expected=false
PASS [user message]: got=false expected=false
PASS [content-block functionCall]: got=true expected=true
8/8 passed, 0 failed
```

Restart recovery test coverage matrix:

| Scenario | Detected | Idempotency Guard |
|----------|----------|-------------------|
| assistant-tail with raw `tool_calls[]` | ✅ | ✅ injected |
| assistant-tail with content-block `toolCall` | ✅ | ✅ injected |
| assistant-tail with content-block `toolUse` | ✅ | ✅ injected |
| assistant-tail with content-block `functionCall` | ✅ | ✅ injected |
| user message with string `content` | ✅ | text extracted |
| user message with content-block `[{type:"text",...}]` | ✅ | text extracted |
| user message with multi-modal content blocks | ✅ | text extracted |
| assistant-tail text-only | N/A | omitted |
| no user message in transcript | N/A | recovery skipped |
| approval-pending tail | N/A | recovery skipped |

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. `hasAssistantToolCalls()` correctly identifies pending tool calls in both raw and canonical content-block formats
  2. `findLastUserMessage()` extracts text from user messages with string content, text-only content blocks, and multi-modal content blocks (text + image_url)
  3. The idempotency guard message is injected when tool calls are detected in any format
  4. All 42 tests pass, including 4 new regression tests for content-block formats (2 assistant-side, 2 user-side)

- **What was not tested**: Live gateway restart scenario with real tool execution (mocked gateway call in tests). Both detectors are verified at the source level with all canonical message shapes.

## Root Cause

N/A — this is a feature enhancement. The prior behavior (fail-closed on non-resumable assistant tail) was intentional but incomplete. The content-block format gaps were because the original implementation only handled raw API message shapes, missing the OpenClaw canonical representations produced by normalization.

## Idempotency Guard (ClawSweeper P1 addressed)

### Commit `ecef1dd0`: Canonical content-block tool call detection

The `hasAssistantToolCalls()` function now detects pending tool calls in **both** formats:

1. **OpenAI raw format**: `msg.tool_calls[]` — checked first
2. **OpenClaw content-block format**: `msg.content[]` blocks with type `toolCall`, `toolUse`, or `functionCall` — matches `agents/tool-call-id.ts` `TOOL_CALL_TYPES`

### Commit `2bfa8992`: Content-block user message text extraction

`findLastUserMessage()` now extracts text from user messages in **both** formats:

1. **String content**: `"hello"` — existing behavior
2. **Content-block arrays**: `[{type: "text", text: "hello"}, {type: "image_url", ...}]` — reuses `collectTextContentBlocks()` from `agents/content-blocks.ts`

When pending tool calls are detected, `buildResumeMessage()` injects:
> IMPORTANT: Before taking any action, review the transcript to determine which tool calls from the interrupted turn were already completed. Do not re-execute completed side effects.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target: `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`
- Covered scenarios: 42 tests total, including:
  - Canonical content-block `toolCall` detection → idempotency guard injected
  - Canonical content-block `toolUse` detection → idempotency guard injected
  - Raw `tool_calls[]` detection → idempotency guard injected
  - Content-block user message (multi-modal: text + image_url) → text extracted, recovery proceeds
  - Content-block user message (text-only block) → text extracted, recovery proceeds
  - String user message → text extracted (existing behavior)
  - Text-only assistant tail → no idempotency guard
  - No user message → recovery skipped
  - Approval-pending tail → recovery skipped

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users whose sessions were interrupted at an assistant-tail boundary after a gateway restart will now see their last request re-presented in the transcript with an automatic continue prompt. This works for both plain-text and multi-modal user messages. When the interrupted turn had pending tool calls, the agent is explicitly instructed to review the transcript for already-completed side effects before acting.

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No — the idempotency guard is a prompt-level instruction
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: assistant-tail with content-block toolCall, assistant-tail with content-block toolUse, user message with multi-modal content blocks, user message with text-only content blocks, user message with string content, assistant-tail text-only, no-user-message orphan transcript
- Edge cases checked: mixed content blocks (text + toolCall), mixed content blocks (text + image_url), empty content array, functionCall type, user-role message (should not match as assistant)
- What you did NOT verify: Live gateway restart scenario with real tool execution and side-effect replay

## Best-fix Verdict

Both content-block fixes reuse existing canonical helpers (`TOOL_CALL_TYPES` vocabulary from `tool-call-id.ts`, `collectTextContentBlocks()` from `content-blocks.ts`), keeping the detection in sync with OpenClaw's normalization layer.

- **Best fix**: Yes — the content-block checks complement the raw format checks and reuse the same canonical type vocabulary
- **Refactor needed**: No
- **Alternative considered**: Inline text extraction — rejected in favor of `collectTextContentBlocks()` which is the canonical helper

## AI Assistance 🤖 (required for AI-assisted PRs)

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest-risk area**: The idempotency guard is a prompt-level instruction, not a runtime enforcement gate. An agent could still ignore it and re-execute completed tool calls.
- **Mitigation**: The idempotency instruction is explicit and placed at the start of the recovery message. For the scope of this PR (assistant-tail recovery), the risk of side-effect replay is lower than for tool-result tails because the assistant hasn't yet received tool results to act on.
- **Compatibility impact**: None — existing resumable-tail paths are unchanged. The new behavior only activates for previously-failed non-resumable assistant tails.

---

Related to #95609
target.author: xydt-tanshanshan

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
